heading: Disposals to foreign nations
label: 8677 Disposals to foreign nations
notes: References in TextThe Arms Export Control Act, referred to in subsec. (a), is , , , as amended. Chapter 6 of that Act is classified generally to subchapter VI (§ 2796 et seq.) of chapter 39 of Title 22, Foreign Relations and Intercourse. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 7307 of this title2006—Subsec. (a). substituted “disposal of that vessel, or of a vessel of the class of that vessel, is authorized” for “disposition of that vessel is approved” and inserted at end “In the case of an authorization by law for the disposal of such a vessel that names a specific vessel as being authorized for such disposal, the Secretary of Defense may substitute another vessel of the same class, if the vessel substituted has virtually identical capabilities as the named vessel. In the case of an authorization by law for the disposal of vessels of a specified class, the Secretary may dispose of vessels of that class pursuant to that authorization only in the number of such vessels specified in that law as being authorized for disposal.”Pub. L. 109–3641999—Subsec. (b)(1)(A). substituted “and the Committee on Armed Services” for “and the Committee on National Security”.Pub. L. 106–651996—Subsec. (b)(1)(A). substituted “Committee on Armed Services of the Senate and the Committee on National Security of the House of Representatives” for “Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and House of Representatives”.Pub. L. 104–106

children.heading: .—Larger or Newer Vessels
children.label: a .—Larger or Newer Vessels
children.text: A naval vessel that is in excess of 3,000 tons or that is less than 20 years of age may not be disposed of to another nation (whether by sale, lease, grant, loan, barter, transfer, or otherwise) unless the disposal of that vessel, or of a vessel of the class of that vessel, is authorized by law enacted after . A lease or loan of such a vessel under such a law may be made only in accordance with the provisions of chapter 6 of the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.) or chapter 2 of part II of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.). In the case of an authorization by law for the disposal of such a vessel that names a specific vessel as being authorized for such disposal, the Secretary of Defense may substitute another vessel of the same class, if the vessel substituted has virtually identical capabilities as the named vessel. In the case of an authorization by law for the disposal of vessels of a specified class, the Secretary may dispose of vessels of that class pursuant to that authorization only in the number of such vessels specified in that law as being authorized for disposal.August 5, 197422 U.S.C. 279622 U.S.C. 2311

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of the Navy notifies the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate and the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives in writing of the proposed disposition; and

children.label: B
children.text: 30 days of continuous session of Congress have expired following the date on which such notice is sent to those committees.

For purposes of paragraph (1)(B), the continuity of a session of Congress is broken only by an adjournment of the Congress sine die, and the days on which either House is not in session because of an adjournment of more than 3 days to a day certain are excluded in the computation of such 30-day period.
